spec_match.rationale: Uses `caret-animation: manual` with three distinct state-driven keyframe pulses — applies the spec to a real UX pattern.

interactive.rationale: Three inputs you actually focus to see different pulse patterns.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises three inputs with three pulse patterns; page delivers them.

edge_cases.rationale: Real form validation triggers UX states dynamically — page sets them statically per input, not in response to interaction.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, accent colours drive the pulses, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Wire state to actual events
openQuestions.detail: Right now the input class is hard-coded. Real form UX flips state on blur (invalid) or submit (saved). Add live validation and a submit button.

openQuestions.title: Show alongside ARIA live regions
openQuestions.detail: A pulsing caret is invisible to screen readers. Pair with aria-live='polite' announcements so the demo is also accessible.

openQuestions.title: Verify chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: Add chromestatus.com/feature/5082469066604544 to see-also if absent (invariant 3).

summary: Charming pattern demo — caret as state cue. Static state classes undermine the realism; wire to actual form events and pair with ARIA so it teaches the complete pattern.
